article_title: QuantWare Raises $178 Million Series B to Build Industrial-Scale Quantum Processors, Announces 10,000-Qubit Architecture
summary: All content verified — allowlist warnings are domain classification only, not editorial issues

content_preview.title: QuantWare Raises $178 Million Series B to Build Industrial-Scale Quantum Processors, Announces 10,000-Qubit Architecture
content_preview.summary: Dutch quantum chip firm QuantWare closes a record $178M Series B to scale its VIO processor platform and open KiloFab, the world's largest dedicated quantum foundry.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

QuantWare, the Dutch quantum processor company spun out of TU Delft's QuTech research institute, announced on May 5, 2026 that it has raised $178 million (€152 million) in a Series B round — the largest private funding round ever raised by a dedicated quantum processor company, according to [QuantWare's announcement](https://quantware.com/news/quantware-raises-178-million). The round was heavily oversubscribed and attracted new backers Intel Capital, In-Q-Tel (IQT), and ETF Partners...

recommendations: Consider adding quantware.com, electronicsweekly.com, and tamradar.com to the source allowlist

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive News article on QuantWare's $178M Series B. Good structure with Overview, What We Know (three sub-sections), What We Don't Know, and Analysis. Word count (957) is within News range. Analysis section is well-reasoned and balanced.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0 (quantware.com press release): Confirmed $178M ($152M EUR), May 5 2026 date, oversubscribed round, all investors named (Intel Capital, IQT, ETF Partners, FORWARD.one, Invest-NL, InnovationQuarter Capital, Ground State Ventures, Graduate Ventures), 50+ customers/20 countries, VIO-40K 10,000-qubit/100x claim, 20x production capacity KiloFab, all executive quotes verified verbatim. source-1 (thequantuminsider.com): Kike Miralles full quote confirmed. source-2 (siliconangle.com): Miralles second quote confirmed; TSMC comparison framing confirmed. source-3 (electronicsweekly.com): Rijlaarsdam 'intractable challenges' quote confirmed verbatim. source-4 (tamradar.com): 40,000 signal lines/15x15cm module, A-Line/D-Line product lines, NVQLink, 2028 VIO-40K shipment, $18.33B by 2034/31.6% CAGR market projection, Aparna Prabhakar/Rami Barends/Charles Marcus advisory roles all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quantitative claims, quotes, and company details verified from source snapshots. The article's attribution of Miralles' first sentence to The Quantum Insider and second sentence to SiliconAngle is technically accurate — both sources have the full quote, but the split attribution is not misleading. No fabrications detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section offers balanced assessment of the TSMC comparison and the technical challenges that remain (decoherence at scale).
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no recent QuantWare coverage in archive.
recommendations: Add quantware.com, electronicsweekly.com, and tamradar.com to source allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, thoroughly sourced funding article. All claims verified. Automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS was triggered by allowlist warnings only — no content issues found. Upgrading to APPROVE.
